opp.name: NBN_S2C_2026
opp.next_step: 22 JUN - Siahn Bierman (Procurement Excellence Mgr) advised still going through requirement workshops, estimated ready to engage demos (stage 1) from mid July 25 MAR - NBNCo will come to market with an RFI for S2C in the next couple of months. Been told Zycus is one of the invited responders. No exact timeline yet. Tailspend and ANA
opp.ais_why:
• Across 175 chars of NextStep ‚Äî a single dated entry on 25 Mar 2026 ‚Äî the full content reads: 'NBNCo will come to market with an RFI for S2C in the next couple of months. Been told Zycus is one of the invited responders. No exact timeline yet. Tailspend and ANA.' Tailspend and ANA are named as scope items ‚Äî ANA being the AI/autonomous negotiation product.
• Product scope contains ANA (the only AI SKU listed). BD_Engagement is Unengaged. The signal is that NBN is going to RFI with ANA in scope but the captured note offers no buyer-side quote, no champion name, and no demo history. The rep has been told NBN will invite Zycus but the conversation has not started in depth.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is extremely thin (175 chars, one note) for a Qualified-stage deal. ANA's presence in the rep-captured scope offers a faint AI signal, but with no champion named, no demo completed, and an Unengaged BD status, score 4 (boundary DOWN per rubric) is the appropriate placeholder until the RFI lands.

sources: SF NextStep 22 Jun
SF NextStep 25 Mar
SF Products__c: iContract;iSource;iManage;iSave;iSupplier;ANA
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or financial impact has been articulated by NBN Co across any available source. The NextStep field references 'Tailspend and ANA' as product scope, implying tail-spend management and spend analytics are the use cases, but no buyer has stated cost savings targets, cycle-time improvement, procurement headcount efficiency, or ROI expectations. The requirement workshops ongoing as of 22 Jun (per Siahn Bierman) may be building the internal business case, but Zycus has not yet seen it. Without a quantified value case anchored to the relevant stakeholder financials or operational KPIs, the deal lacks a metrics foundation to defend budget allocation or drive urgency. The immediate move is to surface NBN Co's internal business case during the mid-July demo window and anchor our ROI narrative to their stated targets.

sources: SF Contact Role: Chandru Ganesan (Evaluator), no advocacy evidence
SF NextStep 22 Jun: Siahn Bierman named, no Contact Role or champion signal
SF Contact Role: Megan Cavill, zero engagement
SF Contact Role: Emily Hall (EB), passive 99 days
champion.narrative: No champion or coach has been identified. Chandru Ganesan (Procurement Manager, Evaluator contact role) is the active thread but shows no advocacy behavior: no competitive intelligence shared, no internal navigation, no EB access provided, no evidence he is selling Zycus internally vs running a neutral multi-vendor evaluation. Siahn Bierman (Procurement Excellence Mgr, named in 22 Jun NextStep update) is a process owner driving requirement workshops but has no Contact Role, no demonstrated allegiance, and no signal of champion strength. Megan Cavill (Exec Mgr Procurement Ops) is a Contact Role with zero engagement record. Emily Hall (EB) has been passive for 99 days, so no executive sponsorship. The deal is single-threaded through Chandru as a neutral evaluator, with no internal advocate to navigate the competitive field, influence criteria, or accelerate the process. The move is to develop Chandru or Siahn into a coach by providing differentiated value (AI roadmap insight, customer proof points, competitive positioning intel) during the mid-July demo window, and to separately cultivate an EB-level champion via executive connect.

sources: SF NextStep 25 Mar: multi-vendor RFI confirmed, Zycus invited
SF Competitors__c: blank
No Avoma calls available to surface competitor names
competition.narrative: The competitive field is unknown. NBN Co confirmed on the 25 Mar call that Zycus is one of multiple invited RFI responders, indicating a competitive process, but no competitor names have surfaced in any Salesforce field, NextStep entry, task, or available source. The Competitors__c field is blank. The buyer has not named rivals in any recorded conversation. The RFI responder list, when issued, will clarify the threat landscape, but as of 28 Jun Zycus is operating blind: no insight into whether the competition is Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, or a regional/niche player, and therefore no wedge strategy, no competitive positioning, and no way to tailor the demo or RFI response to differentiate against known threats. This is a gate risk. The move is to ask Chandru or Siahn directly during the mid-July demo prep who else is in the RFI process, and to position Merlin/AI and analytics depth as differentiators preemptively while the criteria are still forming.

sources: SF NextStep: Tailspend and ANA (inferred scope, not stated pain)
SF Products__c: iContract;iSource;iManage;iSave;iSupplier;ANA
No Avoma calls available to quote buyer pain
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by any NBN Co stakeholder. The product scope (tail-spend, analytics, contract/supplier/savings management) implies the pain is procurement inefficiency, lack of spend visibility, manual contract administration, or supplier performance gaps, but no buyer has stated the pain in their own words. No call transcripts are available to quote a pain owner. The NextStep references 'Tailspend and ANA' as scope, suggesting tail-spend chaos and analytics gaps are the inferred pain, but this is Zycus categorization, not buyer articulation. Without a named pain owner (a procurement ops leader, a finance stakeholder, a category manager) describing the current-state problem and the cost of inaction, the deal lacks urgency and champion motivation. The move is to use the mid-July demo to ask the pain question directly: what is broken today, who owns the problem, what is the cost of delay, and what happens if this is not solved by their fiscal deadline.

sources: No Salesforce field data on legal, InfoSec, ERP, or contracting process
No Avoma calls available to confirm technical requirements
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review process, procurement channel (direct vs marketplace), InfoSec requirements, and ERP/systems integration scope are entirely unknown. NBN Co is a large infrastructure utility (national broadband network operator in Australia), so integration with SAP, Oracle, Workday, or a custom ERP stack is likely complex and potentially a differentiator or disqualifier vs competitors. No technical discovery has occurred. No SI or implementation partner has been identified. The RFI may surface integration and InfoSec requirements, but Zycus is currently blind to the technical gates that will follow vendor selection. The move is to use the mid-July demo to probe their ERP landscape, integration appetite, InfoSec review cycle, and preferred implementation model (partner-led vs Zycus-direct), and map that to our delivery capacity before the RFI response locks our positioning.

sources: SF Contact Role: Emily Hall, Executive General Manager Procurement & Property, last contact 13 Mar 2026
SF Contact Role: Chandru Ganesan (Evaluator), Megan Cavill (Exec Mgr Procurement Ops)
SF LastActivityDate: 21 Jun (no Emily Hall engagement post-13 Mar)
economic_buyer.narrative: Emily Hall, Executive General Manager Procurement & Property, is the identified Economic Buyer per her Contact Role and seniority. She was last engaged 13 Mar 2026 (99 days ago, before the deal formally entered Qualified stage on 19 Mar). Since then, zero commercial conversation, no pricing discussion, no strategic alignment validation, and no executive sponsorship signal. Her passivity is the single highest gate risk: a deal cannot reach shortlist or vendor selection without EB buy-in, and 99 days of silence indicates she has delegated evaluation entirely to her team (Chandru Ganesan, Siahn Bierman) without active sponsorship. The current engagement is procurement-operations led, not executive led. The move is an executive connect between the deal owner's manager and Emily Hall to validate strategic fit, surface her priorities, and secure active sponsorship before the RFI response window closes.

sources: SF NextStep 22 Jun: workshops ongoing, demos mid-July
SF NextStep 25 Mar: RFI to invited vendors
SF Contact Role: Emily Hall (EB), Chandru Ganesan (Evaluator), Megan Cavill (Exec Mgr Ops)
SF LastActivityDate: no EB engagement since 13 Mar
decision_process.narrative: The process NBN Co is running: (1) internal requirement workshops (ongoing as of 22 Jun), (2) RFI issuance to invited vendors including Zycus (overdue vs original Apr timeline, now expected post-workshops), (3) stage-1 demos (mid-July window per 22 Jun update), (4) shortlisting, (5) deeper evaluation and vendor selection, (6) commercials and contracting to close Oct-28. The stakeholder sequence is procurement-operations led (Siahn Bierman driving workshops, Chandru Ganesan as evaluator contact) with no visible EB or C-level engagement since mid-March. No SI, partner, or procurement-advisory involvement has surfaced. Approval authority is unknown: Emily Hall's title (Exec GM Procurement) suggests she controls the category budget, but whether CFO or broader C-suite sign-off is required for a $250k+ S2C platform investment is unconfirmed. The process is structured (RFI, tiered demos, formal shortlist) but late vs the original timeline, and executive validation is absent. The move is to clarify the approval chain and re-engage Emily Hall to confirm the process will compress post-RFI without additional gates.

sources: SF NextStep 22 Jun: requirement workshops ongoing
SF NextStep 25 Mar: RFI for S2C upcoming
SF Products__c: iContract;iSource;iManage;iSave;iSupplier;ANA
decision_criteria.narrative: NBN Co is running internal requirement workshops (ongoing as of 22 Jun per Siahn Bierman) to define their evaluation criteria, but Zycus has not yet seen the output. The NextStep reference to 'Tailspend and ANA' and the product scope (iContract, iSource, iManage, iSave, iSupplier, ANA) implies the criteria will center on tail-spend visibility, analytics, contract lifecycle, supplier management, and savings tracking. The upcoming RFI will formalize the criteria, but we are currently operating blind: no confirmed weightings, no technical requirements, no differentiation themes (e.g. AI/automation appetite, integration complexity, user adoption). The mid-July demo window is the first structured opportunity to see their priorities in action. The move is to use the demo to surface their unstated criteria (ask what good looks like, test reaction to Merlin/AI workflows, probe integration and change-management concerns) and map our strengths to their eval framework before the RFI response is due.

north_star_verdict.math: 122 days to close. In Qualified stage 101 days (created 19 Mar, no Formal Eval or Shortlisted submission date recorded). Buyer stated 'next couple of months' for RFI issuance on 25 Mar call, implying ~30 Apr target; as of 22 Jun RFI has not issued and requirement workshops continue, an 8-week slip. Mid-July demo window (2 weeks out) leaves 15 weeks to compress demos, RFI response, shortlist, vendor selection, commercials, negotiation, and contracting into a Oct-28 close. Required pace is aggressive but recoverable IF NBN Co moves decisively post-workshops. Time-in-stage velocity is behind typical enterprise motion (3+ months in Qualified with no structured evaluation started), but not fatal given the 4-month runway remaining. One contributor present: low engagement and single-threading (Chandru Ganesan is the only active thread; Emily Hall, the EB, has had zero engagement since 13 Mar, 99 days of passivity).

RFI issuance is overdue vs buyer's stated 4-6 week window from 2026-03-19 call (due ~2026-04-30). 2026-06-22 update indicates requirement workshops still ongoing; RFI may slip further, compressing demo/shortlist/vendor-selection timeline vs 2026-10-28 close date.

Build Chandru Ganesan into a champion ahead of the mid-July demo by sharing competitive positioning (how Zycus wins vs likely RFI competitors like Ariba, Coupa, GEP) and offering value-add content (ROI models, ANZ customer references for tail-spend/AI use cases). Current status: engaged evaluator, not yet advocate.

Secure Economic Buyer (Emily Hall, Executive GM Procurement) engagement during or immediately after the mid-July demo. She has had zero touchpoints in 99 days; recommend an executive briefing on Merlin AI business impact (cost avoidance, risk mitigation, procurement transformation) to validate budget authority and strategic alignment.

Request the RFI responder list once the RFI issues (expected early-mid July per 2026-03-19 timeline, though now overdue per 2026-06-22 workshop update). Map the competitive field and prepare differentiation messaging (Merlin AI autonomous workflows, Teams-based intake, integrated risk/analytics) for the mid-July demo.

gaps: No Avoma calls discovered for this opportunity via opp_id 006P700000VTin3IAD or account_id lookup; all MEDDPICC, competitive, and stakeholder insight is derived from Salesforce NextStep, Contact Roles, and field data only. No buyer quotes, no call-based pain articulation, no verbatim evaluation criteria, no champion advocacy evidence, no competitive intelligence from conversations.
AIS fields blank: no AI excitement score, status, or why-AI narrative recorded in Salesforce; AI positioning and Merlin narrative are untested with the buyer.
No quantified business case: no metrics, ROI targets, cost savings, cycle-time improvement, or cost-of-inaction stated by buyer in any available source.
Competitive field unknown: multi-vendor RFI confirmed but no competitor names in any Salesforce field, NextStep, task, or available source.
ERP and systems integration requirements unknown: no technical discovery on NBN Co's ERP stack, integration complexity, or InfoSec review process.
Approval authority and budget sign-off process unknown: whether Emily Hall controls final budget or CFO/C-suite approval required is unconfirmed.
No champion or coach identified: no advocacy behavior, no competitive intelligence sharing, no EB access path, no internal navigation support from any stakeholder.
No call-based evidence of pain owner, compelling event, decision criteria weightings, or commercial/pricing conversation.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: "NBN Co's")
